author: Kevin Indig
title: LLM traffic is shrinking
content: Kevin Indig discusses how LLM referral traffic is declining and presents the thesis that LLMs are a visibility surface, not traffic you can bank on. The article reveals that ChatGPT showed fewer citations due to the model router, contributing to declining LLM referral traffic. Key insight: ChatGPT is more like TikTok than Google Search, where the currency of the AI world is visibility rather than click-through traffic. The research emphasizes that marketers should shift their mindset from viewing LLMs as a direct traffic channel to understanding them as a visibility and authority-building platform. This challenges earlier predictions about LLM traffic growth and forces a strategic recalibration of how brands approach AI search optimization. [Note: Full article is paywalled on Substack. Content extracted from publicly available summaries and search results.]
